title: Pinterest Earnings: Will They Beat Expectations Again?
text: Pinterest was preparing to announce third-quarter 2020 results after market close on October 28. The image-sharing platform had demonstrated strong momentum, with shares climbing 158% year-to-date through mid-October. Wall Street anticipated revenue of approximately $377.7 million, representing a 35% year-over-year increase from Q3 2019's $279.7 million. Management had guided for mid-30% range revenue growth. Analysts projected adjusted earnings per share would double to $0.02. The company had previously exceeded analyst expectations in Q2, beating revenue estimates by roughly $17.5 million. That performance sparked a 36% stock surge. The platform reported 416 million MAUs in Q2, up 39% annually. Pandemic-driven increases in home time had provided substantial user growth tailwinds. ARPU had declined 21% year-over-year to $0.70 due to reduced advertiser spending during pandemic-related business closures. Analysts anticipated fourth-quarter revenue would reach $528.8 million (32% growth) with adjusted EPS reaching $0.18. Market reaction would likely depend more on forward guidance than reported quarterly results, as investors focus on demonstrating sustained growth momentum.
